children.name: Nautilus belauensis
children.description: The Palau nautilus is a nautiloid mainly found off of Palau in the Western Carolines. It can be found on fore reef slopes, at depths of 95-504 m (311-1,653'), though typically preferring a range of 150-300 m (492-984'), where water temperatures stay around 16.6 °C (61.88°F) and do not go much lower than 9.4 °C (48.92°F). belauensis are highly mobile, epibenthic scavengers and opportunistic predators which rely mostly on scent for finding food.
